li.p: To find the y-intercept, compute F(0)={7\cdot 0\mathopen{}\left(0-4\right)}=0\text{.} So the y-intercept is {\left(0,0\right)}\text{.}
The quadratic function is given in factored form. We can see that subbing in x=0 or x=4 would result in the output being 0\text{.} So the x-intercepts are (0, 0) and (4, 0)\text{.}
